### [p301_c0013] 混合公開聽證會的日期、時間和地點
[p301_c0014] 2026 年 2 月 3 日(星期二)
[p301_c0015] 聽證會將於 6:00 pm 開始
[p301_c0016] 發言人報名時間: 5:30 pm 開始; 6:30 pm 結束
[p301_c0017] MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
### [p301_c0018] 交通指南
[p301_c0019] 乘坐 Subway: 乘坐地鐵前往 ④④ Bowling Green, ⑥⑥ Whitehall Street, ① South Ferry 或 ①① Broadway.
[p301_c0020] 乘坐 Bus: M15, M15 SBS, M20, M55, SIM1, SIM1C, SIM4C
[p301_c0021] 乘坐 Staten Island Ferry: 乘坐 Staten Island Ferry 至 South Ferry Terminal
### [p301_c0022] 公開聽證會形式
[p301_c0023] 這將是一場混合公開聽證會, 您可選擇親自參加以及通過 Zoom 的在線平台和電話會議功能參加, 屆時活動將在 MTA 2026 年基本建設項目聽證會網站上進行直播:
[p301_c0024] https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing .
### [p301_c0025] 公開聽證會發言報名方式
[p301_c0026] 有意在聽證會上發言的人士需提前報名, 每位報名發言人的發言時間為兩(2)分鐘。
[p301_c0027] 如需報名發言, 您可以: 1) 線上報名, 訪問網址:
[p301_c0028] https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing , 或 2) 撥打公開聽證會熱線 (646) 252-6777
[p301_c0029] (客戶服務代表的工作時間為每天 6 am 至 10 pm) 或 3) 親臨聽證會現場登記。書面意見可通過上述任一方式提交, 如需諮詢額外信息也可通過上述方式聯繫。報名將於 2026 年 1 月 2 日開始, 2026 年 2 月 3 日 6:30 pm 結束。所有書面意見將在 2 月 3 日 6:30 pm 前提交。意見將被轉錄, 並構成本次聽證會的永久記錄的一部分。
### [p301_c0030] 如需查閱完整的 MTA 項目計劃
[p301_c0031] 您可在現場和 MTA 網站 https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 上找到一份符合該提案資助資格的项目完整列表。如需獲取完整 MTA 項目計劃的翻譯版本, 請於 2026 年 2 月 3 日(星期二)之前與我們聯繫。
[p301_c0032] 您還可通過 MTA 網站或下列地址聯繫 MTA, 獲得有關公眾參觀地點和符合資助資格的项目的信息。MTA 也將在 FTA 支付 2026 財政年度的撥款後通過其網站 https://mta.info/transparency/grant-management 公布 5307 章資助項目的最終列表。
### [p301_c0033] 其他提交意見或申請信息的方式
[p301_c0034] 郵寄: 欲提交意見、補充信息或要求將項目申請和最終列表的複印件提交至 FTA, 請致信: MTA Capital Program Funding/Grants Management, RE: 2026 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
### [p301_c0035] 無障礙和語言幫助服務
[p301_c0036] 公開聽證會提供美國手語 (ASL) 和 CART 字幕服務。
[p301_c0037] 聾人或聽力障礙人士可使用您首選的中繼服務或免費的 711 中繼服務, 聯系公開聽證會熱線 (646) 252-6777。
[p301_c0038] 盲人或患有視力障礙的人士如需合理便利安排, 請於 2026 年 1 月 26 日或之前, 通過網站 https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 在線提交申請, 或撥打公開聽證會熱線 (646) 252-6777。
[p301_c0039] 無網絡訪問條件的人士可撥打 Zoom 網絡研討會電話 1-646-828-7666, 輸入網絡研討會 ID: 160 726 7386, 以井號 (#) 鍵結束。
### [p301_c0040] 如需語言幫助或其他便利安排:
[p301_c0041] 請在聽證會日期前至少五個工作日, 通過線上、電話或郵寄方式提交申請。
[p301_c0042] Metropolitan Transportation Authority
[p301_c0043] www.mta.info

## Page 303

### [p303_c0001] 중앙일보 2026년 1월 8일 목요일
### [p303_c0002] The Korea Daily New York
### [p303_c0003] 전면광고 5
### [p303_c0004] 자본 사업 공청회
[p303_c0005] Metropolitan Transportation Authority는 MTA New York City Transit과 그 자회사들, 즉 MTA Long Island Rail Road, MTA Metro-North Railroad, MTA Construction & Development 및 MTA Bus Company (통칭하여 "MTA")를 대표하여, 연방 회계연도 2026년에 대한 MTA의 연방 보조금 신청안에 관해 의견을 수렴하고자 공청회를 개최할 예정입니다. 본 공청회는 대면 참석과 Zoom의 온라인 플랫폼 및 전화회의 기능을 통한 비대면 참석을 모두 허용하는 하이브리드 방식으로 진행되며, MTA 2026 자본 사업 공청회 웹사이트에서 실시간 중계도 제공됩니다.
[p303_c0006] MTA는 U.S. Department of Transportation 산하 Federal Transit Administration(FTA)의 권한에 따라 United States Code의 Title 49/49 U.S.C. 5300 이하 Sections 5307, 5309, 5310, 5337, 5339, 5340과 Bipartisan Infrastructure Law(Surface Transportation Act 및 기타 연방 지출증안법)에 의거하여 연방 자금을 지원받고 있습니다. New York 주 주지사, 지방 공무원, 대중교통 서비스를 운영하는 공공기업은 MTA를 해당 보조금의 수령 기간으로 지정하였습니다.
[p303_c0007] 요청 자금은 아래 명시된 자본 사업 범주에 사용될 예정이며, 그 세부 내용은 MTA Proposed Program of Projects에 보다 상세히 설명되어 있습니다. MTA Program of Projects는 https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 에 접속하거나 아래 기재된 연방차로 전화하여 확인할 수 있습니다. MTA는 특정 교통 서비스 제공업체의 의견 제출을 적극 권장하고 이를 수렴합니다. 이 프로그램에 따른 자본 개선은 일반적으로 MTA 네트워크 내 또는 주 정부나 시 소유의 부지에서 이루어집니다. 부지 취득 또는 이전은 관련 법률과 규제 요건에 따라 수행됩니다. 본 공청회에 포함된 사람들은 승인된 2020-2024년 및 2025-2029년 자본 프로그램의 일부입니다.
[p303_c0008] 해당 자본 사업들은 New York 대도시권의 Metropolitan Planning Organizations의 승인을 이미 받았거나 받을 예정입니다. 1990년 Clean Air Act 개정안에 따라 요구되는 State Implementation Plan(SIP)을 준수합니다. 이 사업들은 환경에 중대한 부정적 영향을 미칠 것으로 예상되지 않습니다. FTA가 제안된 사업 중 어느 하나에 대해 공식 환경영향평가서를 작성하는 경우, MTA는 해당 평가서를 공개하기 위한 공고를 발표할 예정입니다. 고객자 및 장애인과 관련된 모든 법적 요건은 충족될 것입니다.
[p303_c0009] 사업 비용과 연방 보조금 간의 차액은 다음에 나열된 하나 이상의 재원으로부터 확보되는 자금으로 다음에 것으로 예상됩니다. 주 정부, 지방 정부 및 관계 기관의 자금(MTA Bridges and Tunnels 또는 MTA), 연방법(23 U.S.C. 120 (j))에 따라 통행료 수입으로 발생한 비연방 사업 분담금에 대한 인정액(credits), 또는 매각 대금이나 프로그램 수입.
[p303_c0010] 연방 회계연도 2025년에 MTA는 제5307조 자금 9억 2,540만 달러, 제5337조 시설 상태 유지 자금 12억 2,400만 달러, 그리고 제5339조 Bus 및 Bus 시설 자금 3,060만 달러를 지참받았습니다. 연방 회계연도 2026년의 보조금 배정(apportionment)은 아직 확정되지 않았습니다. 일반적으로 연방 자금은 대부분의 자금 지원 범주에 대하여 20%의 자본 보조금이 지방 분담금으로 충당되어야 합니다.
### [p303_c0011] 사업 범주 / 연방 자금 요청 추정액(단위: 백만 달러)

### [p303_c0013] 하이브리드 공청회 일자, 시간 및 장소
[p303_c0014] 2026년 2월 9일 화요일 공청회는 오후 6:00에 시작합니다. 발표자 등록은 오후 5:30에 시작하여 오후 6:30에 종료됩니다.
[p303_c0015] MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
### [p303_c0016] 오시는 길
[p303_c0017] Subway: ●●● 탑승 후 Bowling Green에서 하차, 또는 ●●● 탑승 후 Whitehall Street에서 하차. 또는 ●●● 탑승 후 South Ferry 또는 ●●● 문해제 Street에서 하차. Bus: M15, M15 SBS, M20, M55, SIM1, SIMIC, SIM4C Staten Island Ferry: Staten Island Ferry 탑승 후 South Ferry Terminal 도착
### [p303_c0018] 공청회 방식
[p303_c0019] 본 공청회는 대면 참석과 Zoom의 온라인 플랫폼 및 전화회의 기능을 통한 비대면 참석을 모두 허용하는 하이브리드 공청회로 진행되며, MTA 2026 자본 사업 공청회 웹사이트 https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 에서 실시간 중계도 제공됩니다.
### [p303_c0020] 공청회 발언 등록
[p303_c0021] 공청회에서 발언하기를 원하는 분들은 사전 등록을 해야 합니다. 등록된 각 발언자에게는 2분의 발언 시간이 주어집니다.
[p303_c0022] 발언 등록을 하려면 1) https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 에서 온라인으로 등록하거나, 2) 공청회 할리인 (646) 252-6777로 전화하거나(전화 시간은 매월 오전 6시부터 오후 10시까지 이용할 수 있습니다), 3) 공청회에서 직접 등록할 수 있습니다. 위에 나열된 방법들을 통해 서면 의견 제출 및 추가 정보 요청도 할 수 있습니다. 등록은 2026년 1월 2일에 시작하여 2026년 2월 3일 오후 6:30에 종료됩니다. 서면 의견은 모두 2월 3일 오후 6:30까지 제출되어야 합니다. 제출된 의견은 기록으로 옮겨져 본 공청회의 영구 기록의 일부로 포함됩니다.
### [p303_c0023] Complete MTA Program of Projects 사본 열람
[p303_c0024] 본 제안에 따라 자금 지원 대상이 되는 사업의 전체 목록은 현장 및 웹사이트 https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 에서 확인할 수 있습니다. Complete MTA Program of Projects의 번역본을 받아 보시려면, 2026년 2월 9일 화요일까지 자히에로 문의하기 바랍니다.
[p303_c0025] 공개 열람 장소 및 자금 지원 대상 사업에 관한 정보는 MTA 웹사이트에서 확인할 수 있습니다. MTA는 MTA에 문의하여 확인할 수 있습니다. FTA가 연방 회계연도 2026년 보조금을 교부하면, MTA는 제5307조 자금의 지원되는 사업과 관련된 최종 사업계획서를 MTA 웹사이트 https://mta.info/transparency/grant-management 에도 공개할 예정입니다.
### [p303_c0026] 추가 의견 제출 또는 정보 요청 방법
[p303_c0027] 운영 의견을 제출하거나, 추가 정보를 요청하거나, 신청서 및 FTA에 제출되어야 하는 최종 사업계획서 사본을 요청하려면, 다음 주소로 서신 발송 요청: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2026 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
### [p303_c0028] 접근성 및 언어 지원 서비스
[p303_c0029] 공청회에서 미국 수화 및 CART 자막 서비스를 이용할 수 있습니다.
[p303_c0030] 청각 장애인이거나 난청이 있는 분들은 선호하는 중계 서비스 또는 무료 TFI 중계 서비스를 이용하여 공청회 할리인 (646) 252-6777으로 연결할 수 있습니다.
[p303_c0031] 시각 장애인이거나 시력이 나쁜 분들은 2026년 1월 26일까지 https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing 에서 온라인으로 신청서를 제출하거나 공청회 할리인 (646) 252-6777번으로 전화하여 편의 서비스를 신청할 수 있습니다.
[p303_c0032] 인터넷에 접속할 수 없는 분들은 Zoom 웹사이트 1-646-828-7666번으로 전화할 다음 웹사이트 ID: 8607267386를 입력하고 우물 정 자(비)를 눌러 공청회에 참석할 수 있습니다.
[p303_c0033] 언어 지원 또는 기타 편의 서비스: 공청회 일자로부터 영업일 기준 최소 5일 전까지 온라인, 전화 또는 우편으로 신청서를 제출해 주시기 바랍니다.
[p303_c0034] _Metropolitan Transportation Authority www.mta.info_

## Page 305

### [p305_c0001] Odyans Piblik
### [p305_c0002] ODYANS PIBLIK POU PWOJÉ ENFRASTRIKTI
[p305_c0003] Metropolitan Transportation Authority, sounon MTA New York City Transit ak filiyali yo: MTA Long Island Rail Road; MTA Metro-North Railroad; MTA Construction & Development; ak MTA Bus Company (nou rele yo ansanm "MTA"), pral òganize yon odyans piblik pou mande kòmanté sou pwopozisyon sibvansyon federal MTA yo pou Ane Fiskal Federal 2026 la. Odyans sa a pral fèt nan yon fòma ibrid, sa ki pèmèt patisipasyon an pèsòn ak vityèl atravè platfòm anliy Zoom ak fonksyon konferans telefònik, epi li pral gen ladan l yon difizyon an dirèk sou sit entènèt odyans Pwojé Enfrastrikti MTA 2026 la.
[p305_c0004] MTA a resevwa lajan federal dapre Federal Transit Administration (FTA) ki pou U.S. Department of Transportation anba Seksyon 5307, 5309, 5310, 5337, 5339 ak 5340 nan Title 49, United States Code (49 U.S.C. 5300 et seq.), ansanm ak Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Surface Transportation Act ak lòt allokasyon federal). Gouvènè Eta Nouyòk la, otorite lokal yo, ak operatè sèvis transpò piblik yo chwazi MTA a kòm moun ki resevwa sibvansyon sa yo.
[p305_c0005] Lajan yo mande a se pou kategori pwojé enfrastrikti ki mansyone pi ba a epi ki dekri pi byen nan MTA Proposed Program of Projects. MTA Proposed Program of Projects a disponib pou w li sou https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing oswa lè w rele nimewo kontak nou bay pi ba a. MTA mande epi ankouraje kòmanté founisè transpò prive yo. Amelyorasyon enfrastrikti nan kad Pwogram sa a ajeneralman fèt nan rezo MTA a oswa sou byen Eta oswa vil la. Acha byen oswa relokasyon yo pral fèt ann akò avèk lwa ak egzijans reglasyon ki enpòtan yo. Pwojé ki nan odyans sa a fè pati Pwogram Enfrastrikti 2020-2024 ak 2025-2029 ki te apwouve yo.
[p305_c0006] Metropolitan Planning Organizations pou reyon metropoliten Nouyòk la te apwouve pwojé enfrastrikti yo, oubyen yo prewa apwouve yo, epi yo koresponn ak State Implementation Plan jan Amanmanan Lwa sou Lè Pwòp 1990 la egzije sa. Yo pa prewa pwojé yo pral gen okenn gwo enpak negatif sou anviwònman an. Si FTA a prepare yon deklarasyon enpak anviwònman fòlè pou nenpòt nan pwojé yo pwopoze yo, MTA a ap pibliye yon avè piblik pou ranm deklarasyon an disponib. Yo pral respekte tout egzijans legal ki gen rapò ak granmoun aje yo ak moun ki andikape yo.
[p305_c0007] Yo prewa kouvri nenpòt diferans ant pri pwojé yo ak montan sibvansyon federal la ak lajan ki disponib nan yon oswa plizyè nan sous ki annapre yo: Lajan ajans leta, lokal, ak afilye (tankou MTA Bridges and Tunnels oubyen MTA); kredi pou pati pwojé ki pa federal ki pwodui nan revni peyaj yo ann akò avèk lwa federal la (23 U.S.C. 120 (i)); oubyen lavant pwopriyete oubyen revni pwogram.
[p305_c0008] Nan Ane Fiskal Federal 2025 la, MTA te resevwa 925.4 milyon dola nan lajan Seksyon 5307 yo, 1.221 milya dola nan lajan Eta Bon Reparasyon Seksyon 5337 yo, ak 30.8 milyon dola nan lajan Bus ak Lokal Bus Seksyon 5339 yo. Repatisyon pou Ane Fiskal Federal 2026 la poko disponib. Anjeneral, pou pi fò kategori finansman, fòk gen yon kontribisyon lokal ki egal a 20 pousan nan lajan federal yo pou asistans enfrastrikti.
### [p305_c0009] KATEGORI PWOJÉ / DEMANN FEDERAL KI ESTIME (Dola an Milyon)

### [p305_c0011] Dat, Lè ak Kote Odyans Piblik Ibrid la ap fèt
### [p305_c0012] Madri 3 fevriye 2026
[p305_c0013] Odyans lan kòmanse a 6:00 PM. Enskripsyon moun ki ap gen pou yo pale yo ap ouvri a 5:30 PM epi ap fèmen a 6:30 PM.
[p305_c0014] MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
### [p305_c0015] Fason pou w ale
[p305_c0016] Pran Subway: Pran 4 ou ale Bowling Green; 6 ou ale Whitehall Street; 1 pou ale South Ferry oswa 9 ou ale Broad Street. Pran Bus: M15, M15 SBS, M20, M55, SIM1, SIM1C, SIM4C Pran Staten Island Ferry: Pran Staten Island Ferry pou ale South Ferry Terminal
### [p305_c0017] Fòma Odyans Piblik
[p305_c0018] Sa a pral yon odyans piblik ibrid ak patisipasyon an pèsòn ak atravè platfòm sou entènèt Zoom ak fonksyon konferans telefònik, ak yon difizyon an dirèk disponib sou sit entènèt odyans Pwojé Enfrastrikti MTA 2026 la https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing .
### [p305_c0019] Enskri pou w pale nan Odyans Piblik la
[p305_c0020] Moun ki enterese pou pale nan odyans piblik la dwe enskri davans. Chak moun k ap pale ki enskri gen de (2) minit pou l pale.
[p305_c0021] Pou w enskri pou w pale ouka 1) enskri anliy sou https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing , oswa 2) rele Liy Dirèk Odyans Piblik la nan (646) 252-6777 (Ajan nan telefòn yo ap disponib sofi 6 a.m. rive 10 p.m. chak jou) oswa 3) enskri an pèsòn nan odyans lan. Ou ka voye kòmanté ekrì epi mande plis enfòmasyon atravè mèm opsyon ki mansyone pi wo a. Enskripsyon ap ouvri 2 janvye 2026, e l ap fèmen a 6:30 p.m. 3 fevriye 2026. Nou dwe voye tout kòmanté ekrì yo anvan 6:30 pm 3 fevriye. Yo pral transkri kòmanté yo epi mete yo kòm yon pati nan dosye pèmanan odyans sa a.
### [p305_c0022] Pou w wè yon kopi pwogram pwojé konplè MTA a
[p305_c0023] Yon lis konplè pwojé ki kalifye pou finansman nan kad pwopozisyon sa a disponib sou plas ak sou sit entènèt MTA a sou https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing . Pou w resevwa yon kopi Pwogram Pwojé Konplè MTA a ki tradui, tanpri kontakte nou anvan madri 3 fevriye 2026.
[p305_c0024] Ou ka jwenn enfòmasyon sou anplasman piblik la ka gade ak sou pwojé ki kalifye pou finansman sou sit entènèt MTA a oubyen lè w kontakte MTA a nan adrès ki pi ba a. MTA pral mete pwogram final pwojé yo disponib tou sou sit entènèt li a sou https://mta.info/transparency/grant-management pou pwojé Seksyon 5307 yo finanse lè FTA a bay sibvansyon federal pou ane fiskal 2026 la.
### [p305_c0025] Lòt fason pou w fè kòmanté oswa mande enfòmasyon
[p305_c0026] Palapòs: Pou w voye kòmanté, pou plis enfòmasyon, oswa pou w mande kopi aplikasyon a ak pwogram final pwojé yo pou w voye bay FTA a, lèt yo dwe ale nan adrès sa a: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2026 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
### [p305_c0027] Aksesiblite ak Sèvis Asistans Lang
[p305_c0028] Nan odyans piblik yo, ap gen sèvis Lang Siy Ameriken ak Soutiraj CART k ap disponib.
[p305_c0029] Pou moun ki soud oswa ki gen pwoblèm pou w tande ka tilize sèvis transmisyon yo prefere a oswa sèvis transmisyon 7/11 gratis la pou yo konekte ak Liy Dirèk Odyans Piblik la nan (646) 252-6777.
[p305_c0030] Pou Moun ki Avèg oswa ki Pa Wè byen ka mande aranjanman nan dat oswa anvan 26 janvye 2026, voye yon demann sou entènèt sou https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing oswa y ap rele Liy Dirèk Odyans Piblik la nan (646) 252-6777.
[p305_c0031] Pou moun ki pa gen aksè ak Entènèt ka antre nan odyans lan lè yo rele reyinyon Zoom nan ki nan nimewo 1-646-828-7666 epi antre ID Reyinyon an: 160 726 7386, epi peze siy dyèz la (#).
[p305_c0032] Pou asistans lang oswa lòt aranjanman: Tanpri voye yon demann omwen senk jou ouvrab anvan dat odyans lan sou entènèt, pa telefòn, oswa pa lapòs.

## Page 307

### [p307_c0001] THE NEW YORK AMSTERDAM NEWS
### [p307_c0002] January 8, 2026 - January 14, 2026 • 11
### [p307_c0003] Public Hearing
### [p307_c0004] CAPITAL PROJECTS PUBLIC HEARING
[p307_c0005] The Metropolitan Transportation Authority, on behalf of MTA New York City Transit and its subsidiaries, MTA Long Island Rail Road, MTA Metro-North Railroad, MTA Construction & Development, and MTA Bus Company (collectively referred to as the "MTA"), will hold a public hearing to solicit comments on MTA's federal grant proposals for Federal Fiscal Year 2026. This hearing will be conducted in a hybrid format, allowing participation both in-person and virtually via Zoom's online platform and conference call feature, and will include a livestream on the MTA 2026 Capital Projects hearing website.
[p307_c0006] The MTA receives federal funds pursuant to the Federal Transit Administration (FTA) of the U.S. Department of Transportation under Sections 5307, 5309, 5310, 5337, 5339 and 5340 of Title 49, United States Code (49 U.S.C. 5300 et seq.), as well as the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Surface Transportation Act and other federal appropriations). The Governor of the State of New York, local officials, and publicly owned operators of mass transportation services have designated the MTA to be the recipient of these grants.
[p307_c0007] The requested funds are for categories of capital projects listed below and more fully described in the MTA Proposed Program of Projects. The MTA's Program of Projects is available for review at https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing or by calling the contact number provided below. The MTA solicits and encourages the comments of private transportation providers. Capital improvements under this Program generally take place within the MTA network or on State or city-owned property. Property acquisitions or relocations will be carried out in accordance with relevant laws and regulatory requirements. The projects included in this hearing are part of the approved 2020-2024 and 2025-2029 Capital Programs.
[p307_c0008] The capital projects have been, or expected to be, endorsed by the Metropolitan Planning Organizations for the New York metropolitan region and comply with the State Implementation Plan (SIP) as required by the Clean Air Act Amendments of 1990. The projects are not anticipated to have any significant adverse environmental impact. If the FTA prepares a formal environmental impact statement on any of the proposed projects, the MTA will issue a public notice to make the statement available. All legal requirements related to senior citizens and individuals with disabilities will be met.
[p307_c0009] It is anticipated that any difference between the projects' cost and the federal grant amount will be covered through funds that are made available by any one or more of the following sources: State, local, affiliated agency funds (such as MTA Bridges and Tunnels or MTA); credits for non-federal project shares generated from toll revenues in accordance with federal statute (23 U.S.C. 120 (i)); or sales of property or program income.
[p307_c0010] In Federal Fiscal Year 2025, MTA received $925.4 million of Section 5307 funds, $1.221 billion of Section 5337 State of Good Repair funds, and $30.8 million of Section 5339 of Bus and Bus Facilities funds. Apportionments from Federal Fiscal Year 2026 are not yet available. Federal funds must generally be matched by a local share contribution for capital assistance of 20 percent for most funding categories.
### [p307_c0011] PROJECT CATEGORIES / ESTIMATED FEDERAL REQUEST (Dollars in Millions)

### [p307_c0013] Date, Time and Location of the Hybrid Public Hearing
[p307_c0014] Tuesday, February 3, 2026
[p307_c0015] Hearing begins at 6:00 P.M.
[p307_c0016] Speaker registration opens at 5:30 P.M. and closes at 6:30 P.M.
[p307_c0017] MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
### [p307_c0018] Directions
[p307_c0019] By Subway: Take the 4 6 to Bowling Green; 3 Q to Whitehall Street; 1 to South Ferry or 4 2 to Broad Street.
[p307_c0020] By Bus: M15, M15 SBS, M20, M55, SIM1, SIMC, SIM4C
[p307_c0021] By Staten Island Ferry: Take the Staten Island Ferry to South Ferry Terminal
### [p307_c0022] Public Hearing Format
[p307_c0023] This will be a hybrid public hearing with in-person participation as well as via Zoom's online platform and conference call feature, with a livestream available on the MTA 2026 Capital Projects hearing website https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing .
### [p307_c0024] Registering to Speak at the Public Hearing
[p307_c0025] Those interested in speaking at the public hearing must pre-register. Each registered speaker will have two (2) minutes to speak.
[p307_c0026] To register to speak you may 1) register online at https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing , or 2) call the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777 (Telephone agents are available from 6 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily) or 3) sign up in person at the hearing. Written comments may be submitted and additional information requested through the same options listed above. Registration opens on January 2, 2026, and closes at 6:30 p.m. on February 3, 2026. All written comments must be submitted by 6:30 p.m. February 3rd. Comments will be transcribed and included as part of the permanent record of this hearing.
### [p307_c0027] To View a Copy of the Complete MTA Program of Projects
[p307_c0028] A complete list of projects eligible for funding under this proposal is available onsite and on the MTA Website at https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing . To receive a translated copy of the Complete MTA Program of Projects, please contact us by Tuesday, February 3, 2026.
[p307_c0029] Information on public viewing locations and on the projects eligible for funding can be found on the MTA website or by contacting the MTA at the address below. The MTA will also make the final program of projects available on its website at https://mta.info/transparency/grant-management for Section 5307 funded projects when the FTA awards the Federal Fiscal Year 2026 grants.
### [p307_c0030] Additional Ways to Comment or Request Information
[p307_c0031] By Postal Mail: To submit comments, for additional information, or to request copies of the application and final program of projects to be submitted to the FTA, letters should be addressed to: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2026 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
### [p307_c0032] Accessibility and Language Assistance Services
[p307_c0033] At the public hearing, American Sign Language (ASL) and CART Captioning services will be available.
[p307_c0034] For Individuals Who Are Deaf or Hard of Hearing can use your preferred relay service or the free 711 relay service to connect to the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777.
[p307_c0035] For Individuals Who Are Blind or Have Low Vision can request accommodations on or before January 26, 2026, submitting a request on line at https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing or by calling the Public Hearing Hotline at (646) 252-6777.
[p307_c0036] For Those Without Internet Access can dial into the hearing by calling the Zoom webinar at 1-646-828-7666 then enter Webinar ID: 160 726 7386, followed by the pound (#) sign.
### [p307_c0037] For Language assistance or other accommodation:
[p307_c0038] Please submit a request at least five business days before the hearing date online, by telephone, or by postal mail.
[p307_c0039] www.mta.info

### [p309_c0006] Общественные слушания
### [p309_c0007] ОБЩЕСТВЕННЫЕ СЛУШАНИЯ НА ТЕМУ КАПИТАЛЬНЫХ ПРОЕКТОВ
[p309_c0008] Metropolitan Transportation Authority от имени MTA New York City Transit и ее дочерних компаний; MTA Long Island Rail Road; MTA Metro-North Railroad; MTA Construction & Development; и MTA Bus Company (совместно именуемые «MTA») проведет публичные слушания с целью сбора комментариев по предложениям MTA в том, что касается федеральных грантов на 2026 финансовый год. Эти слушания будут проводиться в гибридном формате, что позволит участвовать в них как очно, так и виртуально через онлайн-платформу Zoom и функцию конференц-связи, а также будут включать прямую трансляцию на веб-сайте MTA, посвященном слушаниям на тему капитальных проектов 2026 года.
[p309_c0009] MTA получает федеральные средства от Federal Transit Administration (FTA) при U.S. Department of Transportation в соответствии с разделами 5307, 5309, 5310, 5337, 5339 и 5340 Title 49, United States Code (49 U.S.C. 5300 и далее), а также в соответствии Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Surface Transportation Act другие федеральные ассигнования). Губернатор штата Нью-Йорк, местные чиновники и государственные операторы услуг общественного транспорта назначили MTA в качестве получателя этих грантов.
[p309_c0010] Запрашиваемые средства предназначены для перечисленных ниже категорий капитальных проектов, более подробно описанных в MTA Proposed Program of Projects. С программой проектов MTA Program of Projects можно ознакомиться на сайте https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing или позвонив по контактным номеру телефона, указанному ниже. MTA приглашает частных операторов общественного транспорта к участию в обсуждении и приветствует их комментарии. Капитальные улучшения в рамках этой программы, как правило, осуществляются в сети MTA или на территории и объектах, принадлежащих штату или городу. Приобретение или перемещение имущества будет осуществляться в соответствии с действующим законодательством и нормативными требованиями. Проекты, включенные в это слушание, являются частью утвержденных капитальных программ на 2020-2024 и 2025-2029 годы.
[p309_c0011] Капитальные проекты были или, как ожидается, будут одобрены Metropolitan Planning Organizations для городской агломерации Нью-Йорка и соответствуют требованиям State Implementation Plan (SIP), как того требуют поправки к Закону о чистом воздухе 1990 года. Проекты не должны оказать значительного негативного воздействия на окружающую среду. Если FTA подготовит официальное заявление о воздействии на окружающую среду по любому из предлагаемых проектов, MTA опубликует публичное уведомление, чтобы сделать это заявление доступным. Все юридические требования, касающиеся пожилых граждан и лиц с ограниченными возможностями, будут выполнены.
[p309_c0012] Предполагается, что любая разница между стоимостью проектов и суммой федеральной субсидии будет покрыта за счет средств, предоставляемых одним или несколькими из следующих источников: фонды штата, местных органов власти, аффилированных агентств (таких как MTA Bridges and Tunnels или MTA); кредиты на нефедеральные доли проектов, полученные от доходов от сборов в соответствии с федеральным законом (23 U.S.C. 120 (ii)), или продажа имущества или доходы от программ.
[p309_c0013] В 2025 федеральном финансовом году MTA получила 925,4 млн долларов из фондов, предусмотренных разделом 5307, 1,221 млрд долларов из фондов, предусмотренных разделом 5337 «Поддержание надлежащего состояния», и 30,8 млн долларов из фондов, предусмотренных разделом 5339 «Bus и инфраструктура автобусного транспорта». Распределение средств из федерального финансового года 2026 еще не доступно. Федеральные средства, как правило, должны сопровождаться местным софинансированием в размере 20 % для большинства категорий финансирования.
### [p309_c0014] КАТЕГОРИИ ПРОЕКТОВ /ПРЕДПОЛАГАЕМЫЙ ФЕДЕРАЛЬНЫЙ ЗАПРОС СРЕДСТВ (в миллионах долларов)
[p309_c0015]


### [p309_c0016] Дата, время и место гибридных общественных слушаний
[p309_c0017] Вторник, 3 февраля 2026 года.
[p309_c0018] Слушания начнутся в 18:00.
[p309_c0019] Регистрация выступающих начнется в 17:30 и закончится в 18:30.
[p309_c0020] MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004.
### [p309_c0021] Как добраться
[p309_c0022] На Subway: Воспользуйтесь линиями 4 & 5 до Bowling Green; R & W до Whitehall Street; 1 до South Ferry или 2 & 3 до Broad Street.
[p309_c0023] На Bus: M15, M15 SBS, M20, M55, SIM1, SIM1C, SIM4C
[p309_c0024] На Staten Island Ferry: Доезжайте на Staten Island Ferry до South Ferry Terminal
### [p309_c0025] Формат общественных слушаний
[p309_c0026] Это будет гибридное общественное слушание, в котором можно будет принять участие как очно, так и через онлайн-платформу Zoom и с помощью функции конференц-связи, при этом прямая трансляция будет доступна на веб-сайте MTA на тему капитальных проектов 2026 года: https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing .
### [p309_c0027] Регистрация выступающих на общественных слушаниях
[p309_c0028] Все, кто заинтересован выступить на общественных слушаниях, должны пройти предварительную регистрацию. У каждого зарегистрированного выступающего будет две (2) минуты на то, чтобы выступить.
[p309_c0029] Чтобы зарегистрироваться для выступления, вы можете 1) зарегистрироваться онлайн на https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing , или 2) позвонить на горячую линию по вопросам общественных слушаний по номеру (646) 252-6777 (агенты горячей линии доступны с 06:00 до 22:00 каждый день) или 3) зарегистрироваться лично на слушаниях. Письменные комментарии могут быть представлены, а дополнительная информация запрошена с помощью тех же средств, что и указаны выше. Регистрация открывается 2 января 2026 г. и закрывается в 18:30 3 февраля 2026 г. Все письменные комментарии должны быть представлены до 18:30 3 февраля. Комментарии будут записаны и включены в постоянный протокол данного слушания.
### [p309_c0030] Чтобы просмотреть копию полной программы проектов MTA
[p309_c0031] Полный список проектов, имеющих право на финансирование в рамках данного предложения, доступен на месте проведения слушаний и на веб-сайте MTA по адресу https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing . Чтобы получить переведенную копию полной программы проектов MTA, свяжитесь с нами до вторника, 3 февраля 2026 года.
[p309_c0032] Информацию о местах общественного просмотра и о проектах, имеющих право на финансирование, можно найти на веб-сайте MTA или связавшись с MTA по адресу, указанному ниже. MTA также разместит окончательную программу проектов на своем веб-сайте по адресу https://mta.info/transparency/grant-management для проектов, финансируемых в соответствии с разделом 5307, когда FTA выделит гранты на 2026 федеральный финансовый год.
### [p309_c0033] Дополнительные способы подачи комментариев или запроса информации
[p309_c0034] По почте: Для подачи комментариев, получения дополнительной информации или запроса копий заявки и окончательной программы проектов, которые будут представлены в FTA, письмо следует направлять по адресу: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2026 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16.94, New York, NY 10004.
### [p309_c0035] Услуги по обеспечению доступности и языковой поддержки
[p309_c0036] На публичных слушаниях будут доступны услуги американского жестового языка (ASL) и субтитры по системе CART.
[p309_c0037] Лица с полной или частичной потерей слуха могут воспользоваться предпочитаемой службой ретрансляции или бесплатной службой ретрансляции 711 для подключения к горячей линии публичных слушаний по номеру (646) 252-6777.
[p309_c0038] Лица с полной или частичной потерей зрения , могут запросить специальные условия до 26 января 2026 г., отправив запрос онлайн по адресу https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing или позвонив по горячей линии публичных слушаний по номеру (646) 252-6777.
[p309_c0039] Лица, не имеющие доступа к интернету , могут подключиться к слушанию, позвонив по номеру вебинара Zoom 1-646-828-7666 и введя идентификатор вебинара: 160 726 7386, а затем знак решетки (#).
[p309_c0040] Для получения языковой помощи или других мер адаптации для лиц с ограниченными возможностями:
[p309_c0041] Отправьте запрос не позднее чем за пять рабочих дней до даты слушания онлайн, по телефону или по почте.
[p309_c0042] Metropolitan Transportation Authority
[p309_c0043] 1708-20
[p309_c0044] www.mta.info
[p309_c0045] _Master Page # 309 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 311

### [p311_c0001] VOL 35 • ISSUE 1791
### [p311_c0002] WEEKLY BANGALEE
### [p311_c0003] 10 JANUARY 2026
### [p311_c0004] বাংলাদেশ 15
### [p311_c0005] গণ-শুনানি
### [p311_c0006] ক্যাপিটাল প্রজেক্টের গণ-শুনানি
[p311_c0007] Metropolitan Transportation Authority, MTA New York City Transit এবং এর সহযোগী সংস্থা; MTA Long Island Rail Road; MTA Metro-North Railroad; MTA Construction & Development; এবং MTA Bus Company (সম্মিলিতভাবে "MTA" নামে পরিচিত) এর পক্ষে, 2026 সালের ফেব্রুয়ারি অর্থবছরের জন্য MTA-এর ফেডারেল অনুদান প্রস্তাবের উপর মন্তব্য জানাতে একটি জল শুনানি করবে। এই শুনানিটি একটি হাইব্রিড ফর্ম্যাটে পরিচালিত হবে, যার মাধ্যমে Zoom-এর অনলাইন প্ল্যাটফর্ম এবং কনফারেন্স কল বৈশিষ্ট্যের মাধ্যমে সরাসরি এবং ভার্চুয়ালি উভয়ভাবেই অংশগ্রহণ করা যাবে এবং MTA 2026 ক্যাপিটাল প্রজেক্টস শুনানির ওয়েবসাইটে একটি লাইভস্ট্রিম অন্তর্ভুক্ত থাকবে।
[p311_c0008] U.S. Department of Transportation-এর Federal Transit Administration (FTA) অনুসারে, Title 49, United States Code (49 U.S.C. 5300 et seq.) এর ধারা 5307, 5309, 5310, 5337, 5339 এবং 5340, এবং Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (Surface Transportation Act এবং অন্যান্য ফেডারেল বরাদ্দ) অনুসারে MTA ফেডারেল ফান্ড গ্রহণ করে। নিউ ইয়র্ক স্টেটের গভর্নর, স্থানীয় কর্তৃত্বের এবং গণপরিষদের পরিষেবার সরকারি মালিকানাধীন অপারেটররা MTA-কে এই অনুদানের প্রাপক হিসেবে মনোনীত করেছেন।
[p311_c0009] অনুরোধকৃত তহবিলগুলি নিচে তালিকাভুক্ত মূলধন প্রকল্পের বিভাগগুলির জন্য এবং MTA Proposed Program of Projects-এ আরও সম্পূর্ণরূপে বর্ণিত। MTA's Program of Projects পর্যালোচনার জন্য https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing দিকে উপলব্ধ রয়েছে অথবা নিচে দেওয়া যোগাযোগ নম্বরে ফোন করতে জানা যেতে পারে। MTA বেসরকারি পরিষদে সরবরাহকারীদের মতামত কামনা করে এবং উৎসাহিত করে। এই প্রোগ্রামের আওতায় মূলধনী উন্নয়নমূলক কাজ সাধারণত MTA নেটওয়ার্কের ভেতরে অথবা রাজ্য বা শহরের মালিকানাধীন সম্পত্তিতে সম্পন্ন হয়। সম্পত্তি অধিগ্রহণ বা স্থানান্তর প্রাসঙ্গিক আইন এবং নিয়ন্ত্রক প্রয়োজনীয়তা অনুসারে সম্পন্ন করা হবে। এই শুনানিতে অন্তর্ভুক্ত প্রকল্প অনুমোদিত 2020-2024 ও 2025-2029 ক্যাপিটাল প্রোগ্রামের অংশ।
[p311_c0010] মূলধন প্রকল্পগুলি নিউ ইয়র্ক মেট্রোপলিটন অঞ্চলের জন্য Metropolitan Planning Organizations দ্বারা অনুমোদিত হয়েছে, বা প্রত্যাশিত, এবং 1990 সালের ক্লিন এয়ার আইন সংশোধনী অনুসারে State Implementation Plan (SIP) মেনে চলে। প্রকল্পগুলি পরিবেশগতভাবে উল্লেখযোগ্যভাবে কোনও প্রতিকূল প্রভাব ফেলবে বলে আশা করা হচ্ছে না। যদি FTA প্রস্তাবিত কোনও প্রকল্পের উপর একটি আনুষ্ঠানিক পরিবেশগত প্রভাব বিবৃতি প্রস্তুত করে, তাহলে MTA বিবৃতিটি উপলব্ধ করার জন্য একটি পার্লিক নোটিশ জারি করবে। প্রবীণ নাগরিক এবং প্রতিবেশী ব্যক্তিদের সাথে সম্পর্কিত সমস্ত আইনি প্রয়োজনীয়তা পূরণ করা হবে।
[p311_c0011] প্রকল্পের খরচ এবং ফেডারেল অনুদানের পরিমাণের মধ্যে যেকোনো পার্থক্য নিম্নলিখিত যেকোনো এক বা একাধিক উৎস থেকে উপলব্ধ তহবিলের মাধ্যমে পূরণ করা হবে বলে আশা করা হচ্ছে: স্টেট, স্থানীয়, অনুমোদিত সংসদ তহবিল (যেমন MTA ব্রিজস অ্যান্ড টানেল বা MTA); ফেডারেল আইন (23 U.S.C. 120 (i)) অনুসারে টোল রাজস্ব থেকে উৎপন্ন নন-ফেডারেল প্রকল্পের শেয়ারের জন্য কেডিটি; অথবা সম্পত্তি বিক্রয় বা প্রোগ্রামের আয়।
[p311_c0012] 2025 সালের ফেডারেল অর্থবছরে, MTA ধারা 5337 তহবিলের $925.4 মিলিয়ন ডলার, ধারা 5337 স্টেট অফ গুড রিপেয়ার তহবিলের $1.221 বিলিয়ন ডলার এবং Bus ও Bus সুবিধা তহবিলের ধারা 5339 এর $30.8 মিলিয়ন ডলার পেয়েছে। 2026 সালের ফেডারেল অর্থবছরের বর্তন এখনও পাওয়া যায়নি। বেশিরভাগ অর্থায়ন বিভাগের ক্ষেত্রে মূলধনী সহায়তার জন্য প্রাপ্ত ফেডারেল তহবিল সাধারণত 20 শতাংশ স্থানীয় অংশীদারিত্বের মাধ্যমে মেলাতে হয়।
### [p311_c0013] প্রকল্পের শ্রেণিবিভাগ / আনুমানিক ফেডারেল অনুরোধ (মিলিয়ন ডলারে)
[p311_c0014]

```text
Table with 3 columns: Project Category, Amount ($M), and Total ($M). Categories include Long Island Rail Road, Construction & Development, New York City Transit, Metro-North Railroad, MTA Bus Company, and MTA Total.
```
### [p311_c0015] হাইব্রিড গণশুনানির তারিখ, সময় এবং লোকেশন
[p311_c0016] মঙ্গলবার, 3 ফেব্রুয়ারি, 2026 শুনানি শুরু হবে 6:00 P.M. স্পিকার রেজিস্ট্রেশন 5:30 P.M থেকে এবং 6:30 P.M-এ বন্ধ হয়। MTA Headquarters, 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, William J. Ronan Board Room, New York, NY 10004
### [p311_c0017] নির্দেশ
[p311_c0018] Subway দিয়ে: বোলিং গ্রিনে যেতে 4 1/2 নিম; হোয়াইটহল স্ট্রিটে যেতে 4 1/2 নিম; Ferry-তে যেতে 1 অথবা 2 ব্রড স্ট্রিট নিম। Bus-এ করে: M15, M15 SBS, M20, M55, SIM1, SIM1C, SIM4C Statet Island Ferry-র মাধ্যমে: Staten Island Ferry ধরে South Ferry Terminal-এ যান
### [p311_c0019] গণ শুনানির ফরম্যাট
[p311_c0020] এটি একটি হাইব্রিড গণশুনানি হবে যেখানে সরাসরি অংশগ্রহণের পাশাপাশি জমের অনলাইন প্ল্যাটফর্ম এবং কনফারেন্স কল বৈশিষ্ট্যের মাধ্যমেও অংশগ্রহণ করা হবে, যার লাইভস্ট্রিম MTA 2026 ক্যাপিটাল প্রজেক্টস শুনানির ওয়েবসাইটে https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing-এ উপলব্ধ।
### [p311_c0021] গণশুনানিতে বক্তৃতা দেওয়ার জন্য রেজিস্টার করা
[p311_c0022] গণশুনানিতে বক্তব্য রাখতে আগ্রহীদের অবশ্যই রেজিস্ট্রেশনের আগে করতে হবে। প্রতিটি রেজিস্টার করা বক্তা বক্তব্য রাখার জন্য দুই (2) মিনিট সময় পাবেন।
[p311_c0023] বক্তৃতা দেওয়ার জন্য রেজিস্টার করতে আপনি 1) https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing এই টিকানাযুক্ত অনলাইনে রেজিস্টার করতে পারেন, অথবা 2) পাবলিক হিয়ারিং হটলাইনে (646) 252-6777 নম্বরে কল করতে পারেন (টেলিফোন এজেন্টরা প্রতিদিন 6 a.m. থেকে 10 p.m. পর্যন্ত উপলব্ধ) অথবা 3) শুনানিতে ব্যক্তিগতভাবে সাইন আপ করতে পারেন। উপরে তালিকাভুক্ত একই বিকল্পের মাধ্যমে লিখিত মন্তব্য জমা দেওয়া যেতে পারে এবং অতিরিক্ত তথ্যের জন্য অনুরোধ করা যেতে পারে। রেজিস্ট্রেশন শুরু হবে 2 জানুয়ারি, 2026 তারিখে এবং শেষ হবে 3 ফেব্রুয়ারি, 2026 তারিখে 6:30 p.m.-এ। সমস্ত লিখিত মন্তব্য 3 ফেব্রুয়ারি 6:30 p.m-এর মধ্যে জমা দিতে হবে। মন্তব্য কপি করা হবে এবং এই শুনানির স্থায়ী রেকর্ডের অংশ হিসেবে অন্তর্ভুক্ত করা হবে।
### [p311_c0024] সম্পূর্ণ MTA প্রকল্পের প্রোগ্রামের একটি কপি দেখতে
[p311_c0025] এই প্রস্তাবের মধ্যে ফান্ডের জন্য যোগ্য প্রকল্পের সম্পূর্ণ তালিকা অনসাইট এবং MTA ওয়েবসাইটে https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing এ পাওয়া যাবে। সম্পূর্ণ MTA প্রকল্প কর্মসূচির একটি অদৃশ্য কপি পেতে, অনুগ্রহ করে মঙ্গলবার, 3 ফেব্রুয়ারি, 2026 এর মধ্যে আমাদের সাথে যোগাযোগ করুন।
[p311_c0026] জনসাধারণের দেখার জন্য এবং তহবিলের জন্য যোগ্য প্রকল্প সম্পর্কে MTA ওয়েবসাইটে পাওয়া যাবে অথবা নীচের টিকানাযুক্ত MTA-এর সাথে যোগাযোগ করে পাওয়া যাবে। FTA যখন ফেডারেল অর্থবছর 2026 অনুদান প্রদান করবে, তখন MTA তার ওয়েবসাইটে https://mta.info/transparency/grant-management-এ ধারা 5307 অর্থায়িত প্রকল্পের জন্য প্রকল্পগুলির চূড়ান্ত কর্মসূচি উপলব্ধ করবে।
### [p311_c0027] মন্তব্য করার বা তথ্য অনুরোধ করার অতিরিক্ত উপায়
[p311_c0028] ডাকযোগে: মন্তব্য জমা দিতে, অতিরিক্ত তথ্যের জন্য, অথবা FTA-তে জমা দেওয়া প্রকল্পের আবেদনপত্র এবং চূড়ান্ত কর্মসূচির কপির জন্য অনুরোধ করতে, চিঠি নিম্নলিখিত টিকানাযুক্ত পাঠাতে হবে: MTA Capital Program Funding / Grants Management, RE: 2026 Capital Projects Public Hearing, 2 Broadway, D16-94, New York, NY 10004.
### [p311_c0029] অ্যাক্সেসিবিলিটি এবং ভাষা সহায়তা পরিষেবা
[p311_c0030] গণশুনানিতে, আমেরিকান সাইন ল্যাঙ্গুয়েজ (ASL) এবং CART ক্যাপশনিং পরিষেবা উপলব্ধ থাকবে।
[p311_c0031] বধির বা গ্রহণশক্তিহীন ব্যক্তিরা আপনার পছন্দের রিলে পরিষেবা অথবা বিদ্যামূলক 711 রিলে পরিষেবা ব্যবহার করে (646) 252-6777 নম্বরে পাবলিক হিয়ারিং হটলাইনে যোগাযোগ করতে পারেন।
[p311_c0032] অথবা বা কন ট্রান্সক্রিপশন ব্যক্তিরা 26 জানুয়ারি, 2026 তারিখের মধ্যে বা তার আগে https://mta.info/2026-capital-projects-hearing টিকানাযুক্ত অনলাইনে অনুরোধ জমা দিয়ে অথবা (646) 252-6777 নম্বরে পাবলিক হিয়ারিং হটলাইনে কল করে আবাসনের জন্য অনুরোধ করতে পারবেন।
[p311_c0033] যাদের ইন্টারনেট অ্যাক্সেস নেই তারা 1-646-828-7666 নম্বরে জুম ওয়েবিনারে কল করে শুনানিতে যোগ দিতে পারেন এবং তারপর ওয়েবিনার ID লিখুন: 160 726 7386, এরপর পাউন্ড (#) টিক।
### [p311_c0034] ভাষাগত সহায়তা বা অন্যান্য সুবিধার জন্য:
[p311_c0035] শুনানির তারিখের কমপক্ষে পাঁচ কর্মদিবস আগে অনলাইনে, টেলিফোনে, অথবা ডাকযোগে একটি অনুরোধ জমা দিন।

## Page 322

[p322_c0001] 1 prepared statement, which will become part of the hearings public 2 record. I would like to note that a transcript of this hearing 3 will be made and a copy will be supplied to each MTA board 4 member. Before we proceed with the technical details of today's 5 Capital project's public hearing, it is important to note the 6 significance of federal funding and its impact on New York's 7 transit system. The MTA receives both formula and discretionary 8 and competitive grants from the federal government. With an 9 annual allocation of approximately two to two and a half billion 10 dollars in federal support, these funds play a critical role in 11 sustaining and advancing capital projects that keep the region's 12 transportation network operating efficiently. As New York has a 13 long history of being a donor state, contributing more in federal 14 taxes than it receives in funding, this hearing provides an oppo- 15 -- important opportunity to underscore the essential role of 16 federal investment in maintaining and modernizing the MTA's 17 infrastructure. Looking ahead, the approved $68.4 billion 2025 18 to 2029 Capital Program is expected to utilize approximately 14 19 billion in federal funds, accounting for about 20 percent of the 20 programs total funding. These investments support a transit 21 network that carries approximately 43 percent of all US mass 22 transit riders, reinforcing the MTAs national significance and 23 the critical need for continued federal partnership. This year, 24 we are at a particularly important juncture as Congress is 25 working on the reauthorization of surface transportation funding,
[p322_c0002] _6_
[p322_c0003] _Master Page # 322 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 323

[p323_c0001] 1 including Mass Transit, the infrastructure investment in Jobs Act 2 or IIJA, which has been critical in supporting the two to two and 3 a half billion dollars in annual federal funding for the MTA is 4 set to expire later this year. It is essential that the next 5 authorization preserves or enhances these funding levels so the 6 MTA can continue delivering vital capital investments for the 7 region. This hearing is an opportunity for the members of the 8 public to comment on the proposals. Representatives of the MTA 9 are here to listen and record your comments. We will not be 10 responding directly to your comments today. The panelists for 11 tonight are Gillian Persaud for MTA Bus Company, Ralph Barone for 12 Long Island Railroad, Michael McKenna from Metro-North Railroad, 13 Alex Cohen for New York City Transit, and Leah Flax for MTA 14 Construction and Development Company. Also on the panel tonight 15 is Sophia-LaFrance Brooks, who is our hearing officer. I should 16 emphasize, public comments including spoken comments today, all 17 correspondence and e-mails will be transcribed and incorporated 18 into the official record and distributed to every board member. 19 Notice of this hearing was also posted on MTA website along with 20 multilingual translations. The languages translated in addition 21 to English include Chinese, Spanish, Korean, Russian, Bengali, 22 and Haitian Creole. This is a public hearing being held at the 23 Metropolitan Transportation Authority or MTA in connection with 24 the request to the United States Department of Transportation for 25 Federal Financial Assistance under multiple federal legislative

[p324_c0001] 1 acts. [p342_c0001] 1 adults, people with disabilities, and those uncertain 2 medications, including antidepressants. ### [p354_c0003] INTRODUCTION
[p354_c0004] MTA Real Estate: MTA Real Estate ("MTA Real Estate") is comprised of two departments (described below) that are responsible for managing both (i) all real estate operational, transactional and development related matters including and (ii) utility franchise agreements, wherein both (i) and (ii) involve the disposition of real property or interests in real property for the Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA") and each of the MTA's subsidiaries and affiliates, which include: MTA New York City Transit, Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, MTA Metro-North Railroad, MTA Long Island Rail Road, MTA Bridges and Tunnels, MTA Staten Island Railway, MTA Bus, MTA Construction & Development ("MTA C&D"), and MTA Grand Central Madison Operating Company (each, an "MTA Agency", and, collectively with the MTA, the "MTA Agencies"). All real estate related transactional matters (other than real estate development matters and major capital projects ) and utility franchise agreements involving the disposition of real property or interests in real property are managed by the Transactions & Operations/RED group of the MTA. Real estate related development matters involving the disposition of real property or interests in real property are managed by the Transit-Oriented Development group of MTA C&D. References in these Guidelines to the MTA or to the MTA Agency are intended to refer to both MTA and the other MTA Agencies, individually and/or collectively, as the context may require. References in these Guidelines to "MTA Real Estate" are intended to refer to both RED and TOD and "MTA Property" are-is intended to refer to real property or interests therein that are owned, leased or controlled by the MTA or an MTA Agency. In addition to dispositions of real estate, MTA Real Estate is also responsible for granting revocable licenses that permit a licensee to access MTA Property for a limited purpose or use on a non-exclusive basis (as more fully described below).
[p354_c0005] Formatted: Font: Bold, No underline, Underline color: Auto
[p354_c0006] Formatted
[p354_c0007] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0008] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0009] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0010] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0011] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0012] Structure: MTA Real Estate comprises two core groups – (i) Transactions & Operations (including Facilities Management) ("TOM" or "RED") and (ii) Transit-Oriented Development ("TOD"). The TOM group is responsible for all real estate- related transactional matters (other than real estate development matters and major capital projects) for the MTA Agencies (including acquisitions, dispositions, and leasing of MTA Property), tenant management, and management and operations of MTA Facilities. The TOD group is responsible for all real estate-related development matters, whether by an MTA Agency, a government or other public or quasi-public entity and/or a private entity, which affects or involves any existing or to-be acquired MTA Property and/or any existing or proposed transit-related facility or amenity, including acquisitions and dispositions of real property or interests in real property for long-term revenue generation and transportation facilities; major capital projects in conjunction with or enhanced by real estate development; other co-development arrangements with private and public entities including public-private partnerships; and other opportunities for realizing revenue from real estate opportunities and strategic planning.
[p354_c0013] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0014] MTA Real Estate Governance: MTA's Chief Administrative Officer (the "CAO") oversees the RED/TOM functions and works with a team of senior officers. The Chief Real Estate Transactions and Operations Officer (" Managing Director, TOM ")—reporting to the CAO, has primary day-to-day responsibility for managing all TOM functions, with direct oversight for, among others, the Director, Transaction Management (" First Deputy, TOM "), Director, Grand Central Retail Leasing and Management, Director, Tenant Management, Director, Operations, Director, Project Management and Director, Finance & Administration.
[p354_c0015] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0016] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0017] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0018] The Senior Vice President, Transit-Oriented Development (" Managing Director, TOD ")—reporting to the Chief Development Officer (the "CDO") through the Deputy CDO, Planning—has primary day-to-day responsibility for managing all TOD functions with direct oversight for, among others, the Vice President, Planning, Vice President, Transactions and Director, TOD Program Management. (See Figures 25 and 26 for
[p354_c0019] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p354_c0020] Formatted: Font: Bold

## Page 355

### [p355_c0001] Page 2 of 136
[p355_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p355_c0003] RED/TOD organizational charts as of the Effective Date of these Guidelines.)
[p355_c0004] The Chief Administrative Officer is responsible for the MTA's and the MTA Agencies' compliance with and enforcement of these Guidelines, as the Board-designated Real Property Disposition Contracting Officer for the MTA and MTA Agencies.
[p355_c0005] Scope of Guidelines: These Guidelines apply only to dispositions of real property under Sections 2895-2897 of the Public Authorities Law ("PAL"), including leasing-out or sale of real property and grants of interests in real property such as easements and leasehold interests, owned or leased and controlled by the MTA or other MTA Agency. Generally, revocable licenses that permit a licensee to access MTA Property for a limited purpose or use on a non-exclusive basis (e.g., access permits or licenses to install monitoring equipment or other protective equipment in connection with adjacent projects) are not transfers of title to real property or of beneficial interests in real property and thus not a disposal of real property or an interest in real property, subject to PAL Section 2897 and these Guidelines. In addition, the licensing-out of real property for the exclusive use and occupancy by the licensee pursuant to licenses that the MTA or another MTA Agency may terminate at will in its sole and absolute discretion upon not more than 60 days' notice without compensation by the MTA or other MTA Agency including repayment of any unamortized tenant improvement costs will be treated as licenses that are not subject to PAL Section 2897 and not a disposition of an interest in real property. Such licensing-out is subject to the MTA Real Estate's Policies and Procedures for the Licensing of Real Property dated November 29, 2011, as amended. If a license agreement grants to the licensee an exclusive right to use and occupy any MTA Property and provides that the licensee's right of use and occupancy is subject to termination only for specified reasons or upon payment of compensation by the MTA or other MTA Agency, however, then the same shall be treated as a lease and these Guidelines will apply. Similarly, the transfer of MTA Property or an interest in MTA Property to an entity or joint venture or other undertaking with another person or entity by contract, lease or other arrangement that involves MTA Property, and through which the MTA or MTA Agency will continue to own an indirect interest in the property, may be considered a mere change of identity in the form of ownership only—from a direct ownership interest to an indirect one—and thus not a disposal of such property subject to PAL Section 2897 and these Guidelines. References in these Guidelines to "dispositions" are intended to include only transfers of title in real property or of inchoate or beneficial interests in real property which would constitute a "disposal" under PAL Section 2895. These Guidelines are supplemented by the Guidelines for Selection of Tenants for Grand Central Terminal that were adopted by the MTA Board on November 18, 2009, as amended (the "GCT Leasing Guidelines"). In the event of any inconsistency or conflict between these Guidelines and the GCT Leasing Guidelines, with respect to a lease at Grand Central Terminal, the GCT Leasing Guidelines shall govern. A copy of the current GCT Leasing Guidelines is attached hereto as Attachment 1 .
[p355_c0006] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p355_c0007] PAL Section 2897 provides that, as a general rule, any leasing-out or sale of real property can only be undertaken after public advertising for bids and for not less than fair market value. However, these limitations do not apply in certain circumstances, which are specified below. Accordingly, although most dispositions by sale or lease will be made pursuant to the request for proposals process that is described in Chapter III of these Guidelines (the " Lease/Sale RFP Process "), others may be made pursuant to direct negotiations as described in Chapter IV of these Guidelines (the " Lease/Sale Negotiation Process "). Given the inherent complexity and uniqueness of TOD development projects, the long-term nature of the dispositions involved and the fact that such projects typically (although not necessarily) will involve the integration, expansion, modernization and/or co-location of a private development use with existing or contemplated MTA Facilities, TOD will generally follow the procedures identified in these Guidelines, but with modifications as noted herein to better conform to the specific requirements necessary to implement complex TOD development and disposition projects consistent with PAL Section 2897.
[p355_c0008] Formatted: Font: Bold, No underline, Underline color: Auto
[p355_c0009] Formatted: Font: Bold, No underline, Underline color: Auto
[p355_c0010] These Guidelines are intended only for the internal guidance of officers and employees of the MTA. Nothing contained in these Guidelines is intended, and the same shall not be construed, to establish expressly or by
[p355_c0011] Formatted: Justified

## Page 356

### [p356_c0001] Page 3 of 136
[p356_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p356_c0003] implication, or confer upon any person or entity (including any prospective proposers or awardees) any right, privilege, remedy, claim, reliance upon or benefit under, or by reason of, any requirement or provision of these Guidelines for third parties. Except to the extent prohibited by law, any provision of these Guidelines may be waived by the MTA Board (by ratification or otherwise) or by the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of MTA 1 .
[p356_c0004] Lease/Sale RFP Process: The Lease/Sale RFP Process is designed, among other things, to give effect to the following requirements under PAL Section 2897:
- [p356_c0005] • the advertisement for proposals must be made prior to the leasing-out, sale or other disposition of MTA Property, through the methods authorized by these Guidelines; • all proposals must be publicly disclosed at the time and place stated in the advertisement; and • the award must be made with reasonable promptness by notice to the responsible proposer whose proposal will be most advantageous to the State, price and other factors considered; provided however that all proposals may be rejected when it is in the public interest to do so.
[p356_c0006] Formatted: Justified
[p356_c0007] The Lease/Sale RFP Process can be single-step or multi-step as described in Chapter III of these Guidelines.
[p356_c0008] Lease/Sale Negotiation Process: PAL Section 2897 specifies that the Lease/Sale Negotiation Process can only be utilized under the following circumstances:
- [p356_c0009] the fair market value of the property to be leased-out is no greater than an average of $15,000. 00 annually over the term or the sale price does not exceed $15,000, as applicable or proposal prices after advertising are not reasonable, either as to all or some part of the property, or have not been independently arrived at in open competition (even if the fair market value, as applicable in (a) above, exceeds $15,000); or the disposition will be to the State or any political subdivision, and the estimated fair market value of the property and other satisfactory terms of disposal are obtained by negotiation (even if the fair market value, as applicable in (a) above, exceeds $15,000); or under those circumstances permitted by subdivision seven of PAL Section 2897, which include the following: the transferee is a government or other public entity, and the terms and conditions of the transfer require that the ownership and use of the asset will remain with the government or any other public entity; the purpose of the transfer is within the purpose, mission or governing statute of the public authority; or such action is otherwise authorized by law.
[p356_c0010] In the case of (a), (b) or (c) above, the disposition must be for no less than the applicable fair market value. In the case of (d) above, it can, but does not have to, be for less than fair market value. However, if the disposition in the case of (d) is for less than fair market value, then the following information must be
[p356_c0011] _1 A glossary of defined terms used in these Guidelines appears at the end of these Guidelines._

## Page 357

### [p357_c0001] Page 4 of 136
[p357_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p357_c0003] provided to the relevant MTA Agency Board (which must make a written determination based upon such information, that there is no reasonable alternative to the proposed below-market transfer that would achieve the same purpose of such transfer) and the public:
- [p357_c0004] (i) a full description of the asset; (ii) an appraisal of the fair market value of the asset and any other information establishing the fair market value sought by the board; (iii) a description of the purpose of the transfer, and a reasonable statement of the kind and amount of the benefit to the public resulting from the transfer, including but not limited to the kind, number, location, wages or salaries of jobs created or preserved as required by the transfer, the benefits, if any, to the communities in which the asset is situated as are required by the transfer; (iv) a statement of the value to be received compared to the fair market value; (iv) the names of any private parties participating in the transfer, and if different than the statement required by subparagraph (iv) of this paragraph, a statement of the value to the private party; and (v) the names of other private parties who have made an offer for such asset, the value offered, and the purpose for which the asset was sought to be used.
[p357_c0005] An "explanatory statement" is required if a leasing-out or sale or grant of easement rights or other disposition pursuant to the Lease/Sale Negotiation Process meets certain criteria, as described in Chapter IV of these Guidelines. Each such explanatory statement must be transmitted to the New York State Comptroller, Director of the Budget, the Authorities Budget Office, the Commissioner of General Services and the Legislature not less than ninety days in advance of such disposition, and a copy thereof must be kept in the project file.
[p357_c0006] _Master Page # 357 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 358

### [p358_c0001] Page 1 of 136
[p358_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p358_c0003] Formatted
### [p358_c0004] CHAPTER 1- INQUIRIES
[p358_c0005] Scope - This chapter establishes guidelines for responding to verbal and written inquiries made to MTA Real Estate regarding the disposition of MTA Property (" Inquiries ").
[p358_c0006] Responsibilities - MTA Real Estate is responsible for coordinating all responses to Inquiries. MTA Real Estate's responsibilities include researching the ownership status of specific properties, contacting the applicable MTA Agencies and TOD to determine whether property is available or whether there may be development opportunities associated with the property in question, and responding to inquirers.
[p358_c0007] Objective - To provide prompt, courteous and accurate responses to Inquiries.
[p358_c0008] Procedures — Except for Inquiries relating to the leasing of space at Grand Central Terminal and Grand Central Madison, which should be referred to the Director, Grand Central Retail Leasing and Management, and Inquiries relating to development, which should be referred to the Managing Director, TOD, all Inquiries should be referred to the First Deputy, TOM, who should either respond directly or else charge an appropriate deputy with doing so. In the first instance, the First Deputy TOM should ascertain:
- [p358_c0009] (1) precisely what property the inquirer is referring to, and which MTA Agency controls it; (2) whether the relevant MTA Agency owns such property or merely leases it and, in the case of leased property, to what extent subletting of such property is permissible; (3) what use the inquirer proposes to make of the property and whether such use would be compatible with MTA Agency use of any MTA Facilities that adjoin such property; (4) whether MTA MTA Real Estate has already been authorized to dispose of such property or (if not) whether the applicable MTA Agency might be amenable to disposing of such property; (5) what restrictions would need to apply to any such disposition; and (6) whether Transit-Oriented Development should be notified and/or involved in the Inquiry.
[p358_c0010] If the Managing Director, TOM and/or the First Deputy TOM (after consultation with the Managing Director, TOD and/or the First Deputy, TOD) determines that the subject property is available for sale purchase or lease or other disposition either as a TOM transaction or, if applicable, as a potential development transaction through TOD, then the First Deputy TOM or the First Deputy, TOD, as applicable (or such Deputy's designee) should so inform the inquirer, taking care to stipulate that (except as otherwise described in these Guidelines) any such sale or lease may be undertaken only in accordance with the Lease/Sale RFP Process, and should see to it that the inquirer is provided in due course with an opportunity to participate in the Lease/Sale RFP Process with respect to such property. If the First Deputy TOM determines that the
[p358_c0011] _Master Page # 358 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 359

### [p359_c0001] Page 2 of 136
[p359_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p359_c0003] subject property is not available for sale or lease but may be available under a license arrangement that may be revoked by the MTA at will, without cost, on not more than 60 days' notice, the First Deputy TOM they (or such Deputy's designee) should so explain to the inquirer, and in that case the Licensing-Out Guidelines shall apply to such property. Otherwise, the First Deputy TOM (or such Deputy's designee) should inform the inquirer that the subject property is not available on any terms and invite the inquirer to register their interest on MTA Real Estate's website for purposes of alerting the inquirer to other opportunities.
[p359_c0004] For templates for appropriate written responses, see Figures 1-5.

## Page 360

### [p360_c0001] Page 1 of 136
[p360_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p360_c0003] Formatted
### [p360_c0004] CHAPTER II – ASSESSMENT OF OPPORTUNITIES
[p360_c0005] Scope – This chapter establishes guidelines for the assessment of opportunities to sell or lease MTA Property that is not needed for operational purposes.
[p360_c0006] Responsibilities – MTA Real Estate is responsible for encouraging the MTA Agencies to identify MTA Property that is either never going to be needed by the MTA Agency for operational purposes or not expected to be needed for such purposes until sometime in the future; for assessing the marketability of such property for sale, lease or license, as appropriate (taking into account relevant legal constraints, physical conditions and real estate market conditions); and for coordinating with the MTA Agencies to facilitate the disposition of such property or the licensing of such property subject to revocation when and as feasible.
[p360_c0007] Objective – To generate revenues for the MTA, consistent with the fulfillment of the MTA’s operating and transportation purposes.
[p360_c0008] Procedures – In response to Inquiries, on its own initiative and/or at the request of the MTA Agencies, MTA Real Estate should evaluate MTA Properties for their revenue-generating potential. To the extent that MTA Property holds such potential, whether best to realize that potential by sale, lease, grant of easement rights, license, or transfer of development/ air rights to adjacent properties will depend on an assessment of (a) the desirability and feasibility of sub-dividing and physically separating such property from adjoining MTA Property, (b) the extent, if any, to which any MTA Agency may have future operational needs for use of the property or excess development/ air rights associated with the property is or can be envisioned, (c) whether (in any case) it will be necessary or desirable for the MTA Agency to maintain long-term control over the ownership and/or use of the property, (d) whether the MTA Agency owns the property or merely leases it, and the extent, if any, to which disposition of the property is limited by reason of the terms of any lease pursuant to which the MTA Agency controls the property or by reason of any other title encumbrances, (e) or by reason of the acquisition of the property as part of FTA-funded projects, and (fe) the potential revenues and/or benefits to be realized. With respect to each property to be evaluated, the Managing Director, TOM and/or the First Deputy TOM should refer such property to the appropriate Deputy Director, who will designate a Transaction Manager to conduct the evaluation under their supervision.
[p360_c0009] In all cases where an inquiry may have potential for development as a future or existing Transit-Oriented Development matter, including the potential for a transfer of development/ air rights to adjacent property owners, the Managing Director, TOM and/or the First Deputy, TOM, will direct such inquiry to the Managing Director, TOD and/or First Deputy, TOD, to afford MTA TOD with the opportunity to explore the development potential.
### [p360_c0010] Maintenance of Marketable Property List
[p360_c0011] With respect to all MTA Real Estate matters, the Deputy Directors are responsible for maintaining and regularly updating the marketable property list for their respective areas of responsibility. They are also responsible for using MTA Real Estate’s Yardi database to run reports and periodically check active accounts for expiring terms of leased or licensed property to ensure that timely marketing of those opportunities is initiated.
[p360_c0012] _Master Page # 360 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 361

### [p361_c0001] Page 2 of 136
[p361_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
### [p361_c0003] A. Site Analysis
[p361_c0004] The Transaction Manager shall conduct a site inspection, in the case of a TOM transaction, with a representative of Tenant Management (TMU) or RED's Tenant Management Vendor, and in any case with the appropriate personnel of the MTA Agency in order to evaluate the condition of the property, the surrounding area, ascertain what utility services may be available for the site and identify what code compliance concerns may need to be addressed. As a part of this inspection, the Transaction Manager should assess the need for and extent to which improvements might be required, and consult with appropriate personnel of the applicable MTA Agency concerning the feasibility and potential costs of such improvements, and (assuming that the cost of such improvements would be justified by the revenue-generating potential of the property) whether it would be most efficient and cost-effective for the applicable MTA Agency to make such improvements (either in anticipation of rental or license fee income or at the direct cost of a lessee, licensee or purchaser) or for the making and/or cost of such improvements to be left to the purchaser, lessee or licensee. After the site inspection, a site assessment report will be completed, with input from all agency personnel and, in the case of a TOM transaction, TMU/Tenant Management Vendor. The Transaction Manager should also consult with MTA Legal to ensure that the Transaction Manager is aware of any contractual or other legal limitations that would affect the MTA Agency's ability to dispose of the subject property.
[p361_c0005] In order to determine the marketability of a property, the following (where applicable) should be considered:
- [p361_c0006] • Present use/condition of site • Accessibility • Visibility • Surrounding uses • Size, shape and physical characteristics of site • Condition of street and station improvements • Impact of any MTA Agency restrictions • Availability of utility services and feasibility of upgrades or additions • Extent to which improvements are needed, and MTA Agency funds are available to pay for such improvements • Easements, covenants and restrictions and other title encumbrances • Traffic patterns/passenger volume counts - peak and off peak • Existing retail at station • Consumer activity during rush and non-rush hour periods • Condition of neighboring properties • Existing vacancies in the area • New/potential development nearby • Any operating issues noted by Tenant Management • Any operational needs for modernization and/or expansion of existing MTA Facilities • Adequacy of storage space or other existing MTA Facilities • Existing parking or available parking in close proximity • Transportation options including taxi, bus routes, and other public and private intermodal systems • Environmental matters • Whether the property or structure, or portions thereof, falls under the purview of the State Historic Preservation Office (SHPO)
[p361_c0007] _Master Page # 361 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 363

### [p363_c0001] Page 1 of 136
[p363_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p363_c0003] Generally, the leasing-out or sale of MTA Property or the grant of easement rights or transfer of development/air rights constitutes an "action" under the State Environmental Quality Review Act ("SEQRA") and thus is subject to SEQRA's requirements. SEQRA requires review of actions to determine whether they may have a significant adverse environmental impact and, if so, preparation of an environmental impact statement before the action may be approved and undertaken. While most lease renewals will be Type II actions under SEQRA regulations – actions which the State Department of Environmental Conservation has found to have no significant adverse impacts – and thereby exempt from review, and some new leases may also fall within a Type II category, the Transaction Manager should consult with MTA Legal's Deputy General Counsel, Unit Chief – Environmental (the " DGC Environmental ") as early in the process as practical to determine what measures are necessary to ensure SEQRA compliance. Further consultation may be necessary as a proposal develops or changes over time. Board approval resulting in a binding lease or sale or grant of easement rights or transfer of development/air rights in MTA Property cannot occur unless SEQRA requirements have been met either through (a) the action being exempt as a Type II action under SEQRA or falling within the exemption from SEQRA set forth in PAL Sections 1266(11), 1266-c(11) or 553-e(1(b)), as applicable; (b) issuance of a negative declaration, or (c) preparation of a final environmental impact statement. If the leasing-out or sale or other disposition of MTA Property involves any federal action, which is rare for TOM transactions, the leasing-out or sale or other disposition would be subject to National Environmental Policy Act (" NEPA ") in addition to SEQRA.
[p363_c0004] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p363_c0005] Each proposed lease or sale, for a TOM transaction, whether via the RFP Process or the Lease/Sale Negotiation Process, requires preparation of and sign-off on a Pre-RFP Authorization Memo (Figure 9), as further described in Chapters III and IV. Each Pre-RFP Authorization Memo should note the SEQRA type or NEPA status, and required actions, if any, to be taken before the transaction is presented to the MTA Board for approval. No Pre-RFP Authorization Memo is required for TOD transactions, however, a determination of the SEQRA type or NEPA status, and required actions, if any, to be taken before the transaction is presented to the MTA Board for approval, must be made with the assistance of MTA Legal's DGC – Environmental.
[p363_c0006] Formatted: Font: Bold Formatted
[p363_c0007] Formatted: Left
[p363_c0008] Additionally, MTA Legal's Deputy General Counsel, Unit Chief – Finance (the " DGC-Finance ") must review the location(s) being offered in each RFP, with MTA Real Estate providing location-specific information as required, prior to the issuance of the RFP to determine whether there are any tax-exempt bond related tax issues that would arise from private use. Only when MTA Legal's DGC- Finance clears such location and use should it be advertised, unless circumstances require advertisement before clearance is given, in which case the RFP must be subject to cancellation if clearance is not given prior to award. In the case of TOM transactions, the Pre-RFP Authorization Memo contains two (2) boxes which must be checked by the Transaction Manager completing that form indicating that MTA Legal's DGC - Finance reviewed the location and use and has given approval to proceed with the RFP. A copy of MTA Legal's DGC - Finance determination should be kept in the RFP file for all MTA Real Estate matters (i.e., TOM and TOD).
[p363_c0009] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p363_c0010] Prior to issuing an RFP, the TOM Transaction Manager should prepare a Parcel Information Sheet (Figure 6) for each property that such person determines to be marketable. The purpose of the Parcel Information Sheet is to collect information that will ultimately be used to describe the property being disposed of in the RFP. A Parcel Information Sheet may be prepared but will not be required for TOD matters.
[p363_c0011] _Master Page # 363 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 364

### [p364_c0001] Page 1 of 136
[p364_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p364_c0003] Formatted: Right: 0.04"
[p364_c0004] Formatted
[p364_c0005] Each Parcel Information Sheet should include information for all applicable fields. A form of Parcel Information Sheet applicable to all MTA Agencies may be found in the RED/TOD shared folder at S:\RFP Documents and Forms\RFP Boilerplate Forms. The parcel information includes:
- [p364_c0006] • Yard property identification number and address • Block and lot number, if applicable • Site plan • Site description, including approximate size and any unusual attributes • Interest (i.e., lease, license, sale) being offered • Preferred or required uses or any use restrictions • Any desired reservation of rights • Guaranty and Security Deposit requirements • Utility requirements and availability • Improvement requirements and associated cost estimates • Design guidelines (if any) • Transaction Manager site visit date
[p364_c0007] The assessment of potential uses and restrictions on use (including any restrictions on items to be sold at the subject location) should consider the following, if applicable:
- [p364_c0008] • MTA Agency requirements • Restrictions in existing MTA Agency licenses or leases at location • Restrictions and covenants in MTA Agency's deed or recorded and unrecorded other title documents • Land use and zoning regulations and building, fire, health and other like codes, to the extent deemed by MTA Legal's DGC - Environmental to be applicable to the subject facilities • Environmental conditions • Existing mechanical and utility infrastructure and availability • Other restrictions based on existing MTA Agency's facilities or infrastructure at, on, over or below the site, including load restrictions
### [p364_c0009] B. Preliminary Fair Market Value Analysis
[p364_c0010] Prior to ordering an appraisal or while one is being prepared (see Chapter V), the Transaction Manager should, to the best of their ability, seek to estimate the fair market value of the subject property. For such purpose, the Transaction Manager may rely on their knowledge of market conditions, information in the Yard property database, the experience of MTA Real Estate colleagues, informal discussions with appraisers, posted broker listings for comparable properties and/or discussions with brokers; provided, however, that any brokers so contacted should be informed that the inquiry is for information purposes only and that the MTA is not (or is not yet) seeking the assistance of such brokers to locate prospective purchasers or tenants. If an estimate of fair market value has already been obtained, the Transaction Manager should use it to help inform their analysis.
[p364_c0011] _Master Page # 364 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

## Page 365

### [p365_c0001] Page 1 of 136
[p365_c0002] Formatted: Header, Centered, Right: 0", Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 6.34"
[p365_c0003] Formatted
### [p365_c0004] C. Conclusion of Site and Preliminary Fair Market Value Analysis
[p365_c0005] The Transaction Manager should prepare a market analysis work sheet describing their analysis of the property and conclusions regarding the marketability of the property. The Transaction Manager should use such market analysis work sheet to guide their discussions with their Deputy Director, and the discussion should encompass the following:
- [p365_c0006] • Parcel Information Sheet • Review of the market analysis work sheet and explanation of fair market value estimate • Analysis of whether revenue-generating potential or other benefits appears to justify cost and effort to make any required improvements to the property. • Critical assumptions.
[p365_c0007] For potential TOM dispositions, the applicable Deputy Director should, upon conclusion of discussions with the Transaction Manager and resolution of any outstanding issues, then present the Pre-RFP Authorization Memo (with the market analysis work sheet attached) to the First Deputy TOM, for approval. Both the applicable Deputy Director and First Deputy should agree as to whether the property should be marketed for sale or lease or license or other disposition. If they determine to move forward with marketing for sale or lease or other disposition, the First Deputy should authorize the commissioning of valuation of the fair market value of MTA Property or the interest therein being disposed of, if it has not already been commissioned, as provided in Chapter V of these Guidelines. The Pre-RFP Authorization Memo is also approved by the Managing Director TOM.
[p365_c0008] For potential TOD dispositions, a similar market analysis should be performed, and the Transaction Manager should seek and obtain input and concurrence from the affected operating MTA Agency in connection with moving forward with any TOD development RFP as to the potential operational impacts, needs and benefits of the proposed transaction for the operating MTA Agency, the impact on any existing MTA Facilities and any new or expanded MTA Facilities that would be involved. The Transaction Manager should consult with MTA Legal’s Deputy General Counsel, Unit Chief – Real Estate Development & Planning (the “ DGC-Development ”) or MTA Legal’s Deputy General Counsel, Unit Chief – Special Projects (the “ DGC - Special Projects ”), as applicable, the First Deputy, TOD and the Managing Director, TOD in connection with any proposed issuance of a TOD development RFP.
[p365_c0009] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p365_c0010] Formatted: Font: Bold
[p365_c0011] Formatted: Justified
[p365_c0012] _Master Page # 365 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_

### [p366_c0003] CHAPTER III –REQUESTS FOR PROPOSALS (RFPs)
[p366_c0004] Formatted: Justified, Indent: Left: 0", Right: 0", Space Before: 0 pt, After: 4 pt, Line spacing: single, Tab stops: Not at 1.97" + 6.57"
[p366_c0005] Scope - This chapter describes the Lease/Sale RFP Process that will be followed by MTA Real Estate.
[p366_c0006] Responsibilities – Unless use of the Lease/Sale Negotiation Process is authorized, MTA Real Estate is responsible for providing all qualified members of the general public with an opportunity to lease or purchase available properties from the MTA or the MTA Agencies and for selecting tenants, ground lessees or purchasers based on predetermined priorities while ensuring the integrity of the selection process.
[p366_c0007] Objective - To maximize and generate MTA revenues while choosing qualified tenants, ground lessees or purchasers who will meet their obligations and improve the appearance of MTA Facilities and the quality of the amenities offered to the public at such facilities. Additionally, in the case of TOD matters, to monetize and generate revenues from MTA Property assets and interests for transportation purposes through development opportunities with third parties, including transfers of excess development/ air rights, long-term ground leases and easements and joint developments to provide for new, expanded and/or modernized MTA Facilities.
[p366_c0008] Procedures - The Lease/Sale RFP Process is described in the following pages.
### [p366_c0009] A. RFP PUBLICATION
[p366_c0010] _Master Page # 366 of 496 - Finance Committee Meeting 3/23/2026_
